---
title: "ASE Project discrete plot"
author: "Caleb"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
editor_options: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---


# RAW
READ IN DATA

```{R}
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)
# Read and rearrange in one step
rate_names <- c("slow", "med", "fast")


# r <- readRDS("../Data/cluster/discrete/raw/diff_sumvar_strict_001.rds")

# Define job IDs for each tree size
job_ids <- list(
  "50t" = "8558401",
  "100t" = "8561556",  # Replace with actual job ID
  "150t" = "8561712"   # Replace with actual job ID
)

tree_sizes <- c("50t", "100t", "150t")
methods <- c("pre_ord_point_tiebreaker", "pre_ord_sample", "post_ord_point", 
             "post_ord_sample", "no_ace")

# Load all data with different job IDs
raw_results <- list()
for (size in tree_sizes) {
  raw_results[[size]] <- list()
  job_id <- job_ids[[size]]  #  Get job ID for this tree size
  
  for (method in methods) {
    raw_results[[size]][[method]] <- list()
    for(i in 1:100) {
      file_path <- file.path("..", "Data", "cluster", "discrete_crown", size, 
                            "disparity", "raw",
                            sprintf("%s_%s_%03d.rds", job_id, method, i))      
      if(file.exists(file_path)) {
        raw_results[[size]][[method]][[i]] <- readRDS(file_path)
      } else {
        warning("Missing file: ", file_path)
        raw_results[[size]][[method]][[i]] <- NULL
      }
    }
  }
}

metric_names <- names(raw_results[["50t"]][[1]][[1]])  # Get from first available


# Transpose structure: metric -> tree_size -> method -> replicate
raw_results_relisted <- setNames(lapply(metric_names, function(metric) {
  setNames(lapply(tree_sizes, function(size) {
    setNames(lapply(methods, function(method) {
      is_sample_method <- grepl("sample", method)
      
      lapply(raw_results[[size]][[method]], function(rep_data) {
        metric_data <- rep_data[[metric]]
        
        if(is_sample_method) {
          lapply(metric_data, function(rate_data) {
            lapply(rate_data, function(fossil_data) {
              if(is.list(fossil_data) && !is.data.frame(fossil_data)) {
                unlist(fossil_data)
              } else {
                fossil_data
              }
            })
          })
        } else {
          metric_data
        }
      })
    }), methods)
  }), tree_sizes)
}), metric_names)

# Build dataframe with tree_size
raw_results_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(raw_results_relisted), function(metric_name) {
  metric_data <- raw_results_relisted[[metric_name]]
  
  do.call(rbind, lapply(names(metric_data), function(size_name) {
    size_data <- metric_data[[size_name]]
    
    do.call(rbind, lapply(names(size_data), function(method_name) {
      method_data <- size_data[[method_name]]
      
      do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(method_data), function(rep_idx) {
        rep_data <- method_data[[rep_idx]]
        
        if(is.null(rep_data)) return(NULL)
        
        do.call(rbind, lapply(names(rep_data), function(rate_name) {
          rate_data <- rep_data[[rate_name]]
          
          if(is.null(rate_data)) return(NULL)
          
          data.frame(
            replicate = rep_idx,
            all = I(list(rate_data$all)),
            high = I(list(rate_data$fossil_high)),
            mid = I(list(rate_data$fossil_med)),
            low = I(list(rate_data$fossil_low)),
            living = I(list(rate_data$living)),
            rate = rate_name,
            method = method_name,
            metric = metric_name,
            tree_size = size_name,  #  Add tree size
            stringsAsFactors = FALSE
          )
        }))
      }))
    }))
  }))
}))

# Pivot to long format
raw_results_df_long <- raw_results_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"),
    names_to = "preservation_level",
    values_to = "error"
  ) %>%
  unnest(error)

# Factor variables
raw_results_df_long$tree_size <- factor(
  raw_results_df_long$tree_size,
  levels = c("50t", "100t", "150t"),
  labels = c("50 taxa", "100 taxa", "150 taxa")
)

raw_results_df_long$method <- factor(
  raw_results_df_long$method,
  levels = c("pre_ord_point_tiebreaker", "pre_ord_sample", 
             "post_ord_point", "post_ord_sample", "no_ace"),
  labels = c("Pre-ord ASE\n(point + tiebreaker)",
             "Pre-ord ASE\n(dist)", "Post-ord ASE\n(point)", 
             "Post-ord ASE\n(dist)", "No ASE")
)

raw_results_df_long$preservation_level <- factor(
  raw_results_df_long$preservation_level,
  levels = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"),
  labels = c("0%", "5%", "15%", "50%", "100%")
)

raw_results_df_long$rate <- factor(
  raw_results_df_long$rate,
  levels = c("slow", "med", "fast"),
  labels = c("Slow", "Medium", "Fast")
)

raw_results_df_long$metric <- factor(
  raw_results_df_long$metric,
  levels = c("sum_var", "sum_quant", "pairwise"),
  labels = c("Sum of Variances", "Sum of Quantiles", "Mean Pairwise Distance")
)



```


```{R}

# library(ggridges)
# library(ggplot2)
# library(tidyr)
# library(scales)
# colours <- c("Slow" = "#8805A8", "Medium" = "#00B945", "Fast" = "#FFB600")

# ridge_plot <- ggplot(raw_results_df_long, aes(x = error, y = preservation_level, fill = rate)) +
#   stat_density_ridges(
#     alpha = 0.7,
#     scale = 1,
#     rel_min_height = 0.01
#   ) +
#   geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "solid") +
#   facet_grid(metric ~ method) +
#   labs(
#     x = "Relative Disparity Error",
#     y = "Fossil Sampling (%)",
#     fill = "Transition Rate"
#   ) +
#   theme_minimal() +
#   scale_fill_manual(
#     values = colours,
#     labels = c("Slow", "Medium", "Fast")
#   ) +
#   theme(
#     axis.text = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
#     axis.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", color = "black"),
#     legend.position = "bottom",
#     legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
#     legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
#     strip.text = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
#     strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray95", color = "black", linewidth = 0.3),
#     panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.4),
#     panel.grid = element_blank(),
#     panel.spacing = unit(0.3, "cm")
#   ) +
#   scale_x_continuous(
#     breaks = c(-1, -0.5, 0, 0.5, 1),
#     labels = c("-1.0", "-0.5", "0", "0.5", "1.0")
#   ) +
#   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-1, 1))

# ggsave("../Manuscript/figures/ridge_plot_50t_8558401.png", 
#        ridge_plot, width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 700, bg = "white")

# ggsave("../Manuscript/figures/ridge_overall_combined_7904947.png", combined_ridge_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 700, bg = "white")



```



## BOX PLOT

```{r}
# Boxplot version
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

colours <- c("Slow" = "#8805A8", "Medium" = "#00B945", "Fast" = "#FFB600")

boxplot_plot_all <- ggplot(raw_results_df_long, 
                           aes(x = preservation_level, y = error, fill = rate, colour = rate)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    alpha = 0.7,
    # outlier.alpha = 0.1,
    outlier.shape = NA,  # Hide outliers for cleaner plot
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "black", linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_grid(metric ~ method) +  #  Only facet by metric and method
  labs(
    x = "Fossil Sampling (%)",
    y = "Relative Disparity Error",
    fill = "Transition Rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = colours,
    labels = c("Slow", "Medium", "Fast")
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = colours,
    labels = c("Slow", "Medium", "Fast"),
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16, color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 15),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray95", color = "black", linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.3, "cm")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = c(-1, -0.5, 0, 0.25),
    labels = c("-1.0", "-0.5", "0", "0.25")
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1, 0.25))

ggsave("../Manuscript/draft/figures/discrete_boxplot.pdf", device = "pdf", boxplot_plot_all, width = 18, height = 14, dpi = 700, units = "in", bg = "white")

```

## Plot for poster/talks

```{R}



# Filter for only Sum of Variances and Sum of Quantiles
filtered_data <- raw_results_df_long %>%
  filter(metric %in% c("Sum of Variances", "Sum of Quantiles"))

colours <- c("Slow" = "#8805A8", "Medium" = "#00B945", "Fast" = "#FFB600")

boxplot_plot_all <- ggplot(filtered_data, 
                           aes(x = preservation_level, y = error, fill = rate)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    alpha = 0.7,
    outlier.shape = NA,  # Hide outliers for cleaner plot
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_grid(metric ~ method) +  # Only 2 metrics now
  labs(
    x = NULL,  #  Remove x-axis title
    y = NULL,  #  Remove y-axis title
    fill = NULL  #  Remove legend title
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = colours,
    # labels = c("Slow", "Medium", "Fast")
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 15, color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_blank(),  #  Remove all axis titles
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),  #  Remove legend title
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
    strip.text = element_blank(),  #  Remove facet labels
    strip.background = element_blank(),  #  Remove facet background
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.3, "cm")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = c(-1, -0.5, 0, 0.25),
    labels = c("-1.0", "-0.5", "0", "0.25")
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1, 0.25))

boxplot_plot_all

ggsave("../Manuscript/plot_flash_talk.png", width = 16, height = 10, units = "in")











```






## ANOVA stuff

```{R}

library(agricolae)
library(emmeans)
library(multcomp)

# Prepare data for ANOVA
# Take median across samples for sample methods (similar to your previous approach)
raw_results_anova <- raw_results_df_long %>%
  group_by(replicate, rate, method, preservation_level, metric, tree_size) %>%
  summarise(error = median(error, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# Transform for ANOVA (log of absolute values + small constant)
raw_results_anova$abs_error <- abs(raw_results_anova$error)
raw_results_anova$log_abs_error <- log(raw_results_anova$abs_error + 0.001)

# Run ANOVA for each metric separately
metrics <- c("Sum of Variances", "Sum of Quantiles", "Mean Pairwise Distance")

for(metric_name in metrics) {
  cat("\n========================================\n")
  cat("ANOVA for", metric_name, "\n")
  cat("========================================\n\n")
  
  # Filter data for this metric
  metric_data <- raw_results_anova[raw_results_anova$metric == metric_name, ]
  
  # Run ANOVA
  anova_model <- aov(log_abs_error ~ rate * method * preservation_level, 
                     data = metric_data)
  
  # Print ANOVA summary
  print(summary(anova_model))
  
  # Check model diagnostics
  cat("\nModel diagnostics:\n")
  # par(mfrow = c(2,2))
  # plot(anova_model)
  # shapiro.test(residuals(anova_model))  # May fail with large n
  
  # Tukey HSD test for each main effect
  cat("\n--- Rate Rankings ---\n")
  tukey_rate <- HSD.test(anova_model, "rate", group = TRUE)
  print(tukey_rate$groups)
  
  cat("\n--- Method Rankings ---\n")
  tukey_method <- HSD.test(anova_model, "method", group = TRUE)
  print(tukey_method$groups)
  
  cat("\n--- Preservation Level Rankings ---\n")
  tukey_preservation <- HSD.test(anova_model, "preservation_level", group = TRUE)
  print(tukey_preservation$groups)
  
  # Pairwise comparisons for rate × method interaction
  cat("\n--- Rate × Method Interaction ---\n")
  rate_method_means <- emmeans(anova_model, ~ rate * method)
  rate_method_cld <- cld(rate_method_means, alpha = 0.05, Letters = letters)
  print(as.data.frame(rate_method_cld))
  
  # Pairwise comparisons for method × preservation interaction
  cat("\n--- Method × Preservation Interaction ---\n")
  method_pres_means <- emmeans(anova_model, ~ method * preservation_level)
  method_pres_cld <- cld(method_pres_means, alpha = 0.05, Letters = letters)
  print(as.data.frame(method_pres_cld))
  
  # Three-way interaction (may be complex to interpret)
  cat("\n--- Three-way Interaction (Rate × Method × Preservation) ---\n")
  three_way_means <- emmeans(anova_model, ~ rate * method * preservation_level)
  three_way_cld <- cld(three_way_means, alpha = 0.05, Letters = letters)
  print(as.data.frame(three_way_cld))
}

library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)  # Adds p-values to lmer()
library(emmeans)

# Transform for normality (log of absolute values)
raw_results_df_long$abs_error <- abs(raw_results_df_long$error)
raw_results_df_long$log_abs_error <- log(raw_results_df_long$abs_error + 0.001)

# Run LMM for each metric
metrics <- c("Sum of Variances", "Sum of Quantiles", "Mean Pairwise Distance")

for(metric_name in metrics) {
  cat("\n========================================\n")
  cat("Linear Mixed Model for", metric_name, "\n")
  cat("========================================\n\n")
  
  # Filter data for this metric
  metric_data <- raw_results_df_long[raw_results_df_long$metric == metric_name, ]
  
  #  Linear Mixed Model with random effects for replicate and tree_size
  lmm_model <- lmer(log_abs_error ~ rate * method * preservation_level + 
                      (1|replicate) + (1|tree_size), 
                    data = metric_data,
                    REML = FALSE)  # Use ML for likelihood ratio tests
  
  # Print model summary
  print(summary(lmm_model))
  
  # ANOVA table (Type III tests)
  cat("\n--- ANOVA Table (Type III) ---\n")
  print(anova(lmm_model, type = 3))
  
  # Estimated marginal means and Tukey comparisons
  cat("\n--- Rate Rankings ---\n")
  emm_rate <- emmeans(lmm_model, ~ rate)
  print(pairs(emm_rate, adjust = "tukey"))
  print(cld(emm_rate, Letters = letters))
  
  cat("\n--- Method Rankings ---\n")
  emm_method <- emmeans(lmm_model, ~ method)
  print(pairs(emm_method, adjust = "tukey"))
  print(cld(emm_method, Letters = letters))
  
  cat("\n--- Preservation Level Rankings ---\n")
  emm_preservation <- emmeans(lmm_model, ~ preservation_level)
  print(pairs(emm_preservation, adjust = "tukey"))
  print(cld(emm_preservation, Letters = letters))
  
  # Rate × Method interaction
  cat("\n--- Rate × Method Interaction ---\n")
  emm_rate_method <- emmeans(lmm_model, ~ rate * method)
  print(cld(emm_rate_method, Letters = letters, alpha = 0.05))
  
  # Method × Preservation interaction
  cat("\n--- Method × Preservation Interaction ---\n")
  emm_method_pres <- emmeans(lmm_model, ~ method * preservation_level)
  print(cld(emm_method_pres, Letters = letters, alpha = 0.05))
  
  # Three-way interaction
  cat("\n--- Three-way Interaction ---\n")
  emm_three_way <- emmeans(lmm_model, ~ rate * method * preservation_level)
  # Print only a subset to avoid overwhelming output
  print(head(as.data.frame(cld(emm_three_way, Letters = letters)), 20))
}





## so at low fossil sample levels, sample method is best because high uncertainty i.e. 0.55 vs 0.45-> best for sampling.
## At higher fossil sample levels/slow rates, point estimate is best because low uncertainty i.e 0.8 vs 0.2 -> best for point estimate.

## We tend to have low fossil sample levels in the fossil record, thus sample method is best.



library(ggplot2)
library(emmeans)

# Store results for each metric
interaction_results <- list()

for(metric_name in metrics) {
  metric_data <- raw_results_df_long[raw_results_df_long$metric == metric_name, ]
  
  lmm_model <- lmer(log_abs_error ~ rate * method * preservation_level + 
                      (1|replicate) + (1|tree_size), 
                    data = metric_data,
                    REML = FALSE)
  
  # Get estimated marginal means
  emm_method_pres <- emmeans(lmm_model, ~ method * preservation_level)
  
  # Convert to dataframe with confidence intervals
  interaction_results[[metric_name]] <- as.data.frame(emm_method_pres)
  interaction_results[[metric_name]]$metric <- metric_name
}

# Combine all metrics
interaction_df <- do.call(rbind, interaction_results)

# Create interaction plot

filtered_interaction_df <- interaction_df %>%
  filter(metric %in% c("Sum of Quantiles", "Sum of Variances"))



interaction_plot <- ggplot(filtered_interaction_df, 
                           aes(x = preservation_level, y = emmean, 
                               color = method, group = method)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = asymp.LCL, ymax = asymp.UCL), 
                width = 0.2, linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = "Fossil Sampling Level",
    y = "Estimated Marginal Mean (log absolute error)",
    color = "Method",
    title = "Method × Preservation Level Interaction"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text =  element_text(size = 15, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    strip.text = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5)
  )

interaction_plot

ggsave("../Manuscript/figures/method_preservation_interaction.png", 
       interaction_plot, width = 14, height = 8, dpi = 600, bg = "white")





# heatmap_plot <- ggplot(interaction_df, 
#                        aes(x = preservation_level, y = method, fill = emmean)) +
#   geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1) +
#   geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f\n%s", emmean, trimws(.group))), 
#             color = "black", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
#   facet_wrap(~ metric, ncol = 1) +
#   scale_fill_gradient2(
#     low = "#2166ac", mid = "#f7f7f7", high = "#b2182b",
#     midpoint = median(cld_combined$emmean),
#     name = "Estimated\nMarginal Mean"
#   ) +
#   labs(
#     x = "Fossil Sampling Level (%)",
#     y = "Method",
#     title = "Method × Preservation Interaction Heatmap",
#     subtitle = "Values show estimated marginal mean; letters indicate significance groups"
#   ) +
#   theme_minimal() +
#   theme(
#     axis.text = element_text(size = 10, color = "black"),

#     axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
#     strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
#     panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5),
#     legend.position = "right"
#   )

# heatmap_plot












```


# RM AXES


```{R}
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)
# Read and rearrange in one step
rate_names <- c("slow", "med", "fast")


# r <- readRDS("../Data/cluster/discrete/raw/diff_sumvar_strict_001.rds")

# Define job IDs for each tree size
job_ids <- list(
  "50t" = "8558401",
  "100t" = "8561556",  # Replace with actual job ID
  "150t" = "8561712"   # Replace with actual job ID
)

tree_sizes <- c("50t", "100t", "150t")
methods <- c("pre_ord_point_tiebreaker", "pre_ord_sample", "post_ord_point", 
             "post_ord_sample", "no_ace")

# Load all data with different job IDs
rmaxes_results <- list()
for (size in tree_sizes) {
  rmaxes_results[[size]] <- list()
  job_id <- job_ids[[size]]  #  Get job ID for this tree size
  
  for (method in methods) {
    rmaxes_results[[size]][[method]] <- list()
    for(i in 1:100) {
      file_path <- file.path("..", "Data", "cluster", "discrete_crown", size, 
                            "disparity", "rmaxes",
                            sprintf("%s_%s_%03d.rds", job_id, method, i))      
      if(file.exists(file_path)) {
        rmaxes_results[[size]][[method]][[i]] <- readRDS(file_path)
      } else {
        warning("Missing file: ", file_path)
        rmaxes_results[[size]][[method]][[i]] <- NULL
      }
    }
  }
}

metric_names <- names(rmaxes_results[["50t"]][[1]][[1]])  # Get from first available


# Transpose structure: metric -> tree_size -> method -> replicate
rmaxes_results_relisted <- setNames(lapply(metric_names, function(metric) {
  setNames(lapply(tree_sizes, function(size) {
    setNames(lapply(methods, function(method) {
      is_sample_method <- grepl("sample", method)
      
      lapply(rmaxes_results[[size]][[method]], function(rep_data) {
        metric_data <- rep_data[[metric]]
        
        if(is_sample_method) {
          lapply(metric_data, function(rate_data) {
            lapply(rate_data, function(fossil_data) {
              if(is.list(fossil_data) && !is.data.frame(fossil_data)) {
                unlist(fossil_data)
              } else {
                fossil_data
              }
            })
          })
        } else {
          metric_data
        }
      })
    }), methods)
  }), tree_sizes)
}), metric_names)

# Build dataframe with tree_size
rmaxes_results_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(rmaxes_results_relisted), function(metric_name) {
  metric_data <- rmaxes_results_relisted[[metric_name]]
  
  do.call(rbind, lapply(names(metric_data), function(size_name) {
    size_data <- metric_data[[size_name]]
    
    do.call(rbind, lapply(names(size_data), function(method_name) {
      method_data <- size_data[[method_name]]
      
      do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(method_data), function(rep_idx) {
        rep_data <- method_data[[rep_idx]]
        
        if(is.null(rep_data)) return(NULL)
        
        do.call(rbind, lapply(names(rep_data), function(rate_name) {
          rate_data <- rep_data[[rate_name]]
          
          if(is.null(rate_data)) return(NULL)
          
          data.frame(
            replicate = rep_idx,
            all = I(list(rate_data$all)),
            high = I(list(rate_data$fossil_high)),
            mid = I(list(rate_data$fossil_med)),
            low = I(list(rate_data$fossil_low)),
            living = I(list(rate_data$living)),
            rate = rate_name,
            method = method_name,
            metric = metric_name,
            tree_size = size_name,  #  Add tree size
            stringsAsFactors = FALSE
          )
        }))
      }))
    }))
  }))
}))

# Pivot to long format
rmaxes_results_df_long <- rmaxes_results_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"),
    names_to = "preservation_level",
    values_to = "error"
  ) %>%
  unnest(error)

# Factor variables
rmaxes_results_df_long$tree_size <- factor(
  rmaxes_results_df_long$tree_size,
  levels = c("50t", "100t", "150t"),
  labels = c("50 taxa", "100 taxa", "150 taxa")
)

rmaxes_results_df_long$method <- factor(
  rmaxes_results_df_long$method,
  levels = c("pre_ord_point_tiebreaker", "pre_ord_point", "pre_ord_sample", 
             "post_ord_point", "post_ord_sample", "no_ace"),
  labels = c("Pre-ord ASE\n(point + tiebreaker)", "Pre-ord ASE\n(point)", 
             "Pre-ord ASE\n(dist)", "Post-ord ASE\n(point)", 
             "Post-ord ASE\n(dist)", "No ASE")
)

rmaxes_results_df_long$preservation_level <- factor(
  rmaxes_results_df_long$preservation_level,
  levels = c("living", "low", "mid", "high", "all"),
  labels = c("0%", "5%", "15%", "50%", "100%")
)

rmaxes_results_df_long$rate <- factor(
  rmaxes_results_df_long$rate,
  levels = c("slow", "med", "fast"),
  labels = c("Slow", "Medium", "Fast")
)

rmaxes_results_df_long$metric <- factor(
  rmaxes_results_df_long$metric,
  levels = c("sum_var", "sum_quant", "pairwise"),
  labels = c("Sum of Variances", "Sum of Quantiles", "Mean Pairwise Distance")
)



```


## BOX PLOT

```{r}
# Boxplot version
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

colours <- c("Slow" = "#8805A8", "Medium" = "#00B945", "Fast" = "#FFB600")

boxplot_plot_all_rm_axes <- ggplot(rmaxes_results_df_long, 
                           aes(x = preservation_level, y = error, fill = rate, colour = rate)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    alpha = 0.7,
    # outlier.alpha = 0.1,
    outlier.shape = NA,  # Hide outliers for cleaner plot
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "black", linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_grid(metric ~ method) +  #  Only facet by metric and method
  labs(
    x = "Fossil Sampling (%)",
    y = "Relative Disparity Error",
    fill = "Transition Rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = colours,
    labels = c("Slow", "Medium", "Fast")
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = colours,
    labels = c("Slow", "Medium", "Fast"),
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16, color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 15),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray95", color = "black", linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.3, "cm")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = c(-1, -0.5, 0, 0.25),
    labels = c("-1.0", "-0.5", "0", "0.25")
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1, 0.25))

ggsave("../Manuscript/draft/figures/discrete_boxplot_rm_axes.pdf", device = "pdf", boxplot_plot_all, width = 18, height = 14, dpi = 700, units = "in", bg = "white")

```


 ## LMM
```{R}


library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)  # Adds p-values to lmer()
library(emmeans)
library(multcomp)

# Transform for normality (log of absolute values)
rmaxes_results_df_long$abs_error <- abs(rmaxes_results_df_long$error)
rmaxes_results_df_long$log_abs_error <- log(rmaxes_results_df_long$abs_error + 0.001)

# Run LMM for each metric
metrics <- c("Sum of Variances", "Sum of Quantiles", "Mean Pairwise Distance")

for(metric_name in metrics) {
  cat("\n========================================\n")
  cat("Linear Mixed Model for", metric_name, "\n")
  cat("========================================\n\n")
  
  # Filter data for this metric
  metric_data <- rmaxes_results_df_long[rmaxes_results_df_long$metric == metric_name, ]
  
  #  Linear Mixed Model with random effects for replicate and tree_size
  lmm_model <- lmer(log_abs_error ~ rate * method * preservation_level + 
                      (1|replicate) + (1|tree_size) + (1|), 
                    data = metric_data,
                    REML = FALSE)  # Use ML for likelihood ratio tests
  
  # Print model summary
  print(summary(lmm_model))
  
  # ANOVA table (Type III tests)
  cat("\n--- ANOVA Table (Type III) ---\n")
  print(anova(lmm_model, type = 3))
  
  # Estimated marginal means and Tukey comparisons
  cat("\n--- Rate Rankings ---\n")
  emm_rate <- emmeans(lmm_model, ~ rate)
  print(pairs(emm_rate, adjust = "tukey"))
  print(cld(emm_rate, Letters = letters))
  
  cat("\n--- Method Rankings ---\n")
  emm_method <- emmeans(lmm_model, ~ method)
  print(pairs(emm_method, adjust = "tukey"))
  print(cld(emm_method, Letters = letters))
  
  cat("\n--- Preservation Level Rankings ---\n")
  emm_preservation <- emmeans(lmm_model, ~ preservation_level)
  print(pairs(emm_preservation, adjust = "tukey"))
  print(cld(emm_preservation, Letters = letters))
  
  # Rate × Method interaction
  cat("\n--- Rate × Method Interaction ---\n")
  emm_rate_method <- emmeans(lmm_model, ~ rate * method)
  print(cld(emm_rate_method, Letters = letters, alpha = 0.05))
  
  # Method × Preservation interaction
  cat("\n--- Method × Preservation Interaction ---\n")
  emm_method_pres <- emmeans(lmm_model, ~ method * preservation_level)
  print(cld(emm_method_pres, Letters = letters, alpha = 0.05))
  
  # Three-way interaction
  cat("\n--- Three-way Interaction ---\n")
  emm_three_way <- emmeans(lmm_model, ~ rate * method * preservation_level)
  # Print only a subset to avoid overwhelming output
  print(head(as.data.frame(cld(emm_three_way, Letters = letters)), 20))
}



```

## Combine RM AXES and Raw

```{R}

raw_results_df_long$axes_treatment <- "Raw"
rmaxes_results_df_long$axes_treatment <- "Axes Removed"

# Now combine
combined_rm_raw_df <- rbind(raw_results_df_long, rmaxes_results_df_long)

# Optionally make it a factor with specific order
combined_rm_raw_df$axes_treatment <- factor(
  combined_rm_raw_df$axes_treatment,
  levels = c("Raw", "Axes Removed")
)





```
